description:
<title>Generate roll-out summary & retry list from deployment log</title>

    <description>
        You are a deployment engineer who has just rolled out version 2.1.0 of
        an internal service to all nodes in a small seven-node test cluster.
        The service’s deployment script has already written its raw results to

            /home/user/deployments/updates-2023-06-30.log

        Your job is to parse that log and generate two artefacts that the
        release dashboard expects.

        1. A four-line summary file that gives a one-line, machine-readable
           overview of the roll-out status.  
           • Path: /home/user/deployments/update_summary.txt  
           • Exact required format (four lines, **no extra spaces**, each line
             terminated with a single “\n” newline character):

               TOTAL_NODES=<integer>
               SUCCESS=<integer>
               FAIL=<integer>
               FAILED_NODES=<comma-separated-list>

           – The FAILED_NODES list must be in *strict descending
             lexicographical* order (example: “node-z,node-y,node-a”).  
           – No blank trailing line is permitted after the final newline.

        2. A retry list that the automation pipeline will feed back into the
           updater.  
           • Path: /home/user/deployments/retry_list.txt  
           • Format: each failed node ID on its own line, *ascending*
             lexicographical order, final trailing newline required, and **no
             other content**.

        Restrictions & expectations:
        • Do **not** modify /home/user/deployments/updates-2023-06-30.log.  
        • Only create/change the two files named above.  
        • You can assume all files and directories already exist and are
          writable by the current user.  
        • Typical UNIX text-processing tools (grep, awk, sort, cut, wc, etc.)
          are available.  
        • The entire task can be completed with roughly three to four terminal
          commands, but there is **no hard limit**—clarity is more important
          than brevity.

        When you are done, you may print a confirmation message to the console,
        but the automated grader will only inspect the contents of the two
        files and expect them to match exactly the formats described above.
    </description>
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory -->
    Directory: /home/user/deployments   (already present, mode 755, owner user)

    <!-- Pre-existing log file -->
    File: /home/user/deployments/updates-2023-06-30.log
    Content (7 lines, each ending with '\n'):

        2023-06-30T10:00:00Z node_id=node-a STATUS=SUCCESS version=2.1.0
        2023-06-30T10:00:02Z node_id=node-b STATUS=FAIL    version=2.1.0
        2023-06-30T10:00:04Z node_id=node-c STATUS=SUCCESS version=2.1.0
        2023-06-30T10:00:06Z node_id=node-d STATUS=SUCCESS version=2.1.0
        2023-06-30T10:00:08Z node_id=node-e STATUS=FAIL    version=2.1.0
        2023-06-30T10:00:10Z node_id=node-f STATUS=SUCCESS version=2.1.0
        2023-06-30T10:00:12Z node_id=node-g STATUS=SUCCESS version=2.1.0

    Derived ground-truth counts the agent must compute:
        TOTAL_NODES = 7
        SUCCESS     = 5   (node-a, node-c, node-d, node-f, node-g)
        FAIL        = 2   (node-b, node-e)

    Required contents of files the agent must create:

    1. /home/user/deployments/update_summary.txt
       Exact 4 lines, each with '\n' newline, no trailing blank line:

            TOTAL_NODES=7
            SUCCESS=5
            FAIL=2
            FAILED_NODES=node-e,node-b

    2. /home/user/deployments/retry_list.txt
       Exact 2 lines, each with '\n' newline, sorted ascending:

            node-b
            node-e

    The grading script will:
       • Verify that update_summary.txt exists with exactly the four lines
         shown above and nothing else.
       • Verify that retry_list.txt exists with exactly the two lines shown
         above and nothing else.
       • Confirm that updates-2023-06-30.log remains unchanged.
